levels.label: Random Acts
levels.copy: The Growth Experimentation Maturity pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Campaign-Led
levels.copy: Some Growth Experimentation Maturity practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Experimental
levels.copy: Core Growth Experimentation Maturity practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Learning Engine
levels.copy: Growth Experimentation Maturity practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Compounding Growth
levels.copy: Growth Experimentation Maturity is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.measurement-and-metric-quality: Govern the critical funnel and define one primary, one leading, and two guardrail metrics.
recommendations.portfolio-and-prioritisation: Rebuild the backlog around explicit problems and expected value of information.
recommendations.experiment-design: Pre-register hypothesis, population, threshold, sample, duration, and decision rule.
recommendations.learning-velocity: Measure question-to-decision cycle time and maintain a reusable evidence repository.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with three questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
